title: centaurea bombycina subsp. bombycina
intro: Centaurea bombycina subsp. bombycina, also known as Purple Cornflower, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Purple Cornflower include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile remedy for various health concerns.
